creator: rfarmer@predatorlabs.net
text:
On Sat, Dec 25, 2010 at 22:15, Anonymous <swell.k@gmail.com> wrote:
> Anonymous <swell.k@gmail.com> writes:
>
>>>> - and using absolute paths breaks devel/ccache usage
>>>
>>> I'm not sure what you mean here. This works with my patch:
>>>
>>> rfarmer@thinkpad /home/lcvs/ports/java/openjdk6
>>> =A0% make CC=3D/usr/local/libexec/ccache/gcc CXX=3D/usr/local/libexec/c=
cache/g++
>>
>> Try setting it from environment or make.conf. Assignments on command
>> line override even `=3D', not only `?=3D'.
>
> Nevermind, it was about `=3D', not `?=3D'. The latter inherts `CC ?=3D cc=
'
> from sys.mk and *always* ignores `$(COMPILER_PATH)gcc'.
>

In any case your patch seems shorter/cleaner, so hopefully it can be
applied soon.

--=20
Rob Farmer
